div.head: The Secretary of State to the Minister in Persia (Hornibrook)
div.p:
46. Your 63, December 18, 2 p.m., and 64, December 19, 2 p.m.Neither printed. The Department assumes that the Persian Government realizes that, as set
forth in the Department’s telegram No. 35 of November 8, 6 p.m., any monopoly which discriminates between American automotive interests would probably render impracticable the proposed negotiations for a reciprocal trade agreement. If you have any reason to believe that the Persian authorities are not fully aware of this fact, you are authorized in your discretion again to bring it to their attention.
